creator: stb@lassitu.de
text:
(In reply to Mohammad S. Babaei from comment #4)

> $ chown git:git /usr/local/etc/gitea/conf/app.ini
> I guess it should be done by the port, so this is a bug.

No, as a general rule: config files are managed by the system admin, and they should not be writeable by the daemon process.

If you'd like to have gitea be able to modify the config file for you, then by all means set the permissions as you'd like.

Please see pkg-message and /usr/ports/UPDATING when upgrading ports.
